snapshot.scope: Independent Product Planning review binds the exact disabled Pi package install foundation together with the checksum-bound machine-local Skill Registry inventory and revision-CAS disable. Permissioned acquisition ends at installed_disabled; registry summaries remain pathless/body-free and authority-reducing; catalog, enable/runtime, update, uninstall and remaining lifecycle stay open.

reviewers.task: Exact disabled package install and Skill Registry foundation review
reviewers.independence: Product Planning owns only documentation and independently compares the Development-owned package manager, capability adapter and Skill Registry with Permission Broker, lifecycle, supply-chain, recovery, IPC, trace and test contracts.

dimensions.summary: Permission Broker solely authorizes exact install; package bytes and Skill Registry stay machine-local outside the vault, while only bounded package identity and pathless confirmed Skill summaries cross renderer boundaries.
Skill, API, architecture, security, recovery and decision owners agree that acquisition ends at installed_disabled, registry metadata grants no authority, and disable only reduces authority.
PIGE-SKILL-001/002, E8.01/04 and P8 remain planned; renderer lifecycle binding, catalog, enable/runtime, update, rollback and uninstall are explicitly open.
Focused tests cover disclosed package identity, integrity/extraction/recovery and Skill checksum/identity binding, no-follow reads, pathless projection, owner-fenced CAS disable and parsed IPC.
Existing Skill, API, architecture, security, quality and recovery owners absorb both foundations; dependency and service inventories avoid duplicate lifecycle contracts.

verification.command: Independent review of exact disabled Pi package install and Skill Registry foundations
verification.evidence: Review confirms exact permissioned acquisition remains fixed-registry/integrity-bound and disabled, while Skill inventory is checksum-bound, pathless and unable to enable or execute code.

verification.command: npm run verify:docs && npm run verify:decisions && npm run verify:traceability
verification.evidence: Links, document map, decisions and locked claims pass with no Requirement, Exit or Phase promotion.

verification.command: npm run verify:contracts && npm run verify:independent-documentation-review && npm run verify:documentation-leanness
verification.evidence: CON-001 through CON-004, dependency/service inventories and focused package/Skill tests bind both foundations while the union recipe retains published product roots.

coordination.taskTitle: Exact disabled package install and Skill Registry foundations
coordination.compatibility: The candidate adds exact permissioned public-registry package acquisition plus pathless Skill inventory/disable; no secret, runtime execution, enable, catalog, update, uninstall, vault or backup authority is added.
coordination.acknowledgement: Product Planning records only bounded lifecycle prerequisites, keeps Development code/test ownership separate, and leaves Skill/package Requirements, Exits and P8 planned.

nonBlockingRisks: Public npm metadata and archives remain untrusted supply-chain input even after integrity verification; package enable/runtime review remains mandatory and unavailable.
Skill Registry renderer binding, enable/grant integration, uninstall and broader package lifecycle/platform evidence remain open.
